input.headline: Why one California town is moving to ban pickleball

raw.headline: Why one California town is moving to ban pickleball

winner: We tried banning pickleball for two years, and all we got was a bunch of people yelling "let me just get my serve!" at each other across a net. Classic.

candidates.text: Why did the pickleball court get a restraining order? Because it was always *serving* too much trouble!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Puns on 'serving' and 'trouble' are clever and specific to the context of pickleball, with a natural flow and surprising twist."}

candidates.text: We tried banning pickleball for two years, and all we got was a bunch of people yelling "let me just get my serve!" at each other across a net. Classic.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Funny and specific, with a clever twist on the classic 'we tried to ban X' format."}

candidates.text: The town decided to ban pickleball because they were tired of seeing people pickling their balls every weekend.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat clever with the wordplay on 'pickling,' but it's a bit forced and lacks specificity about why the town would be bothered by this. It's not offensive, but it could be more surprising and concise."}

candidates.text: This town's ban on pickleball is pickling my sense of humor!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke uses wordplay with 'pickling' but is somewhat forced and lacks specificity about the town or pickleball ban."}

candidates.text: Why did the California town start banning pickleball? Because they realized it was too hard to hit a bird with!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 4.5, "reason": "Lacks surprise and relevance to the headline; the joke doesn't connect logically with the context of banning pickleball."}
